Lee County Stucco Services

Star Stucco of Austin serves homeowners and property owners across Lee County with stucco installation and repair built to last. Rural properties in this part of Texas face tough conditions, from scorching heat and drought to heavy spring rains and high winds. We build exterior wall systems that stand up to all of it, year after year.

Get a Free Stucco Estimate Call Now: (512) 706-9699

Durable Exterior Wall Systems for Lee County Properties

Lee County stretches across the rolling post-oak savannah east of Austin, where agricultural land, cattle ranches, and small-town life define the character of the community. Properties out here are built to work, and the exterior finishes on those properties need to perform under conditions that would wear out lesser materials. The summer heat is relentless, winter cold fronts bring occasional freezes, and the sandy loam and clay soil shifts with every rain cycle.

Stucco is an excellent choice for Lee County homes precisely because it handles these conditions well when installed with proper technique. Unlike vinyl siding that warps in the heat or wood that rots in humid conditions, a well-built stucco wall sheds water, resists UV damage, and requires minimal maintenance over its decades-long lifespan.

Our team travels throughout Lee County, from Giddings and Lexington to smaller communities like Dime Box and Serbin. We bring the same materials, tools, and expertise to every job regardless of location, and we treat every property as if it were our own.

Stucco Services We Offer in Lee County

Professional stucco and plaster solutions for homes and businesses throughout Lee County.

Stucco Repair

Rural properties across Lee County accumulate wear from weather, livestock, equipment, and simple age. We repair damaged stucco on farmhouses, ranch homes, and outbuildings with durable materials and proven techniques that hold up in exposed, open-land settings where wind and sun are constant.

Learn More

Stucco Installation

New homes and additions in the Giddings and Lexington area benefit from stucco that is installed with rural property challenges in mind. We design our installations to resist the wind-driven rain, extended UV exposure, and temperature extremes that are part of daily life in Lee County.

Learn More

Stucco Crack Repair

Sandy loam topsoil over clay substrates throughout Lee County creates seasonal ground movement that cracks stucco surfaces. We repair these cracks with reinforced systems that bridge minor movement without splitting, keeping your walls sealed against moisture intrusion.

Learn More

Stucco Water Damage Repair

When stucco fails on a Lee County property, water can travel deep into wall cavities before anyone notices. We use moisture meters and visual inspection to trace the damage back to its source, then remove and replace all compromised material before sealing the wall with improved flashing and drainage.

Learn More

Exterior Plaster

A new plaster finish gives aging Lee County homes a fresh, clean appearance that lasts for years. We offer finishes ranging from simple sand float textures to more refined troweled surfaces, all mixed with pigments that resist the fading caused by constant sun exposure on open rural properties.

Learn More

EIFS Repair & Installation

For Lee County homeowners looking to reduce energy costs on properties far from shade trees, EIFS adds meaningful insulation to exterior walls. We install modern drainage EIFS and repair older systems, paying close attention to termination details that are critical in wind-exposed rural settings.

Learn More

Commercial Stucco

Agricultural buildings, small-town storefronts, churches, and community facilities throughout Lee County rely on stucco for durable, low-maintenance exteriors. We handle commercial projects of all sizes with the same quality standards we bring to residential work.

Learn More

Understanding of the specific soil and climate conditions that affect stucco in the post-oak savannah region

Self-sufficient crews that bring all materials, water, and equipment needed for jobs in areas without easy utility access

Respectful of working ranch and farm operations, with scheduling that works around your agricultural calendar

Straightforward communication with a single point of contact from estimate through final walkthrough

Built-to-last installations using commercial-grade materials that perform in exposed, open-land environments

Local Expertise

We understand Lee County soil types, weather patterns, and building codes for every community.

Fully Licensed & Insured

Licensed Texas contractor with full liability and workers' compensation coverage for every Lee County project.

Fast Response

Prompt estimates and scheduling for all Lee County communities. Most projects begin within 1–2 weeks.

Common Stucco Problems We Fix in Lee County

Rural properties in Lee County face stucco challenges that differ from suburban homes closer to Austin. Exposure, age, and limited access to qualified contractors all play a role. These are the problems we see most frequently.

Cracks

The sandy loam soils prevalent across Lee County compact unevenly and shift during the wet-dry cycles that define Central Texas seasons. This ground movement translates directly into stucco cracks, especially on older homes near Giddings and Lexington that were built without modern control joints.

Bubbling or soft stucco

Older farmhouses and ranch homes in Lee County sometimes have stucco applied directly over wood sheathing without a modern moisture barrier. Over decades, moisture migrating through the wall softens the stucco from behind, creating bubbles and spongy sections that crumble when pressed.

Water stains

Properties that rely on well water with high mineral content often develop staining on stucco walls from sprinkler overspray and hose splash. Roof runoff carrying tannins from nearby post-oak trees adds brown streaks that are difficult to remove without professional treatment.

Window and door leaks

Wind-driven rain on exposed rural properties hits walls at angles that sheltered suburban homes rarely experience. This forces water into any gap around window and door frames, and on older Lee County homes, the original sealant has often been gone for years.

Failing patches

When the nearest stucco contractor is an hour away, homeowners sometimes attempt repairs themselves or hire general handymen. These well-intentioned patches rarely bond properly to the existing wall and begin to fail within a season or two, often letting in more water than the original damage.

Separation around trim

The wide temperature swings in Lee County, from below freezing on winter mornings to over 100 degrees on summer afternoons, cause constant expansion and contraction that pulls stucco away from window casings, door frames, and decorative trim elements.

Impact damage

Rural life is hard on exterior walls. Tractor implements, livestock, wind-blown branches from pecan and post-oak trees, and severe hailstorms all leave their mark on stucco surfaces. Without prompt repair, these impact points become entry points for water.

Faded or stained exterior walls

Lee County properties sit in open landscape with little shade protection, subjecting stucco to full-day sun exposure that breaks down pigments faster than in tree-covered neighborhoods. Dust from unpaved roads and agricultural activity adds a layer of grime that further dulls the finish.

How We Work in Lee County

A clear, straightforward process from first call to finished project.

1

Schedule inspection

Contact us to arrange a visit to your Lee County property. We travel to Giddings, Lexington, Dime Box, and all points in between. Just let us know the best way to reach your location and we will be there.

2

Review scope

We inspect every accessible wall surface on your property, testing for moisture, examining cracks, and assessing the overall condition of the stucco system. On larger properties with multiple structures, we prioritize the most critical repairs first.

3

Get estimate

You receive a written estimate that spells out the work to be done, the materials we will use, and the total price. We are upfront about costs and will discuss options if there are ways to phase larger projects to fit your budget.

4

Complete work

Our crew arrives prepared with everything needed to complete the job, minimizing trips and keeping the project on track. We work efficiently, clean up thoroughly, and make sure you are happy with the finished product before we leave your property.
